text: Implementing robust security measures is vital for any web application. One fundamental step is using HTTPS, which encrypts data transferred between a user's browser and the server. This prevents attackers from intercepting sensitive information. Another critical layer is authentication, ensuring that only authorised users can access restricted areas, typically through passwords or multi-factor methods. Developers must also practice strict input validation, checking all user-submitted data to prevent malicious code from being processed. This helps defend against common vulnerabilities like SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS). By combining these practices, developers can significantly reduce the risk of data breaches and system compromise.
